message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# habitatv5 Framework Quick Fixes + UX Improvements

## Context
Habitat's sprint framework (React + Supabase) runs during builder events. The eng review identified code duplication, missing AI rate limiting, and underutilized UX guidance fields. Admin display auth was confirmed already working — no change needed.

## Changes (5 total, in order)

### 1. Extract shared `formatTime` → `src/lib/timerUtils.ts` (new file)
- Identical function duplicated in `SprintTimer.tsx:4-10` and `AdminDisplay.tsx:17-22`
- Create `src/lib/timerUtils.ts` with exported `formatTime(seconds)`
- Import in both files, delete local copies

### 2. Extract shared phase constants → `src/lib/phaseConstants.ts` (new file)
- `phaseLabels` duplicated in `TeamProgressCard.tsx:17-24` and implicitly in `AdminDisplay.tsx`
- Create `src/lib/phaseConstants.ts` with `phaseLabels`, `phaseNames`, `phaseOrder`
- Import in `TeamProgressCard.tsx`, `AdminDisplay.tsx`, `PhaseChecklist.tsx`
- Keep component-specific color maps in place (different formats: CSS classes vs Tailwind)

### 3. Client-side AI rate limiting → `src/hooks/useAIGeneration.ts`
- Add `useRef` for `lastGenerationTime`, enforce 10s cooldown
- Show toast "Please wait Xs before generating again" if too fast
- ~9 lines of new code at top of `generate()` function

### 4. Enrich sprint task guidance → `src/data/sprintTasks.ts` (data-only)
- Add `helperText`, `warningText`, `checklist`, `toolLink`, `subNote` to key tasks
- Components already render all these fields — zero component changes needed
- Phase 1: problem, solution, customer, advantage, validation enriched
- Phase 2: prototype, message, contacts enriched
- Phase 3: pitch enriched
- Phase 4: social enriched

### 5. Server-side AI rate limiting → `supabase/functions/generate-startup-content/index.ts`
- Add in-memory request counter (20 requests/hour per token)
- Insert rate check after body parsing, before API call (~25 lines)
- Returns 429 with friendly error message

## Files Modified
- `src/lib/timerUtils.ts` — NEW
- `src/lib/phaseConstants.ts` — NEW
- `src/components/sprint/SprintTimer.tsx` — remove local formatTime, add import
- `src/pages/AdminDisplay.tsx` — remove local formatTime + phases array, add imports
- `src/components/admin/TeamProgressCard.tsx` — remove local phaseLabels, add import
- `src/components/sprint/PhaseChecklist.tsx` — remove local phaseOrder, add import
- `src/hooks/useAIGeneration.ts` — add cooldown logic
- `src/data/sprintTasks.ts` — enrich task objects with guidance fields
- `supabase/functions/generate-startup-content/index.ts` — add rate limiter

## NOT in scope
- Gemini API integration (future session)
- Batch AI reviews for admin (future session)
- Auth migration to Supabase Auth (future, before residency)
- Automation track task enrichment (separate track)

## Verification
- Run `npm run dev`, navigate sprint phases — timer displays correctly
- Open `/admin/display` — timer + phase dots render
- Open admin dashboard — team cards show correct phase labels/colors
- Click AI generate, then immediately again — see cooldown toast
- Check enriched tasks show helper text, warnings, checklists in sprint view


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/canerden/.claude/projects/-Users-canerden/1bba2b6d-c76c-483a-b16f-5f4264864a52.jsonl

planContent:
# habitatv5 Framework Quick Fixes + UX Improvements

## Context
Habitat's sprint framework (React + Supabase) runs during builder events. The eng review identified code duplication, missing AI rate limiting, and underutilized UX guidance fields. Admin display auth was confirmed already working — no change needed.

## Changes (5 total, in order)

### 1. Extract shared `formatTime` → `src/lib/timerUtils.ts` (new file)
- Identical function duplicated in `SprintTimer.tsx:4-10` and `AdminDisplay.tsx:17-22`
- Create `src/lib/timerUtils.ts` with exported `formatTime(seconds)`
- Import in both files, delete local copies

### 2. Extract shared phase constants → `src/lib/phaseConstants.ts` (new file)
- `phaseLabels` duplicated in `TeamProgressCard.tsx:17-24` and implicitly in `AdminDisplay.tsx`
- Create `src/lib/phaseConstants.ts` with `phaseLabels`, `phaseNames`, `phaseOrder`
- Import in `TeamProgressCard.tsx`, `AdminDisplay.tsx`, `PhaseChecklist.tsx`
- Keep component-specific color maps in place (different formats: CSS classes vs Tailwind)

### 3. Client-side AI rate limiting → `src/hooks/useAIGeneration.ts`
- Add `useRef` for `lastGenerationTime`, enforce 10s cooldown
- Show toast "Please wait Xs before generating again" if too fast
- ~9 lines of new code at top of `generate()` function

### 4. Enrich sprint task guidance → `src/data/sprintTasks.ts` (data-only)
- Add `helperText`, `warningText`, `checklist`, `toolLink`, `subNote` to key tasks
- Components already render all these fields — zero component changes needed
- Phase 1: problem, solution, customer, advantage, validation enriched
- Phase 2: prototype, message, contacts enriched
- Phase 3: pitch enriched
- Phase 4: social enriched

### 5. Server-side AI rate limiting → `supabase/functions/generate-startup-content/index.ts`
- Add in-memory request counter (20 requests/hour per token)
- Insert rate check after body parsing, before API call (~25 lines)
- Returns 429 with friendly error message

## Files Modified
- `src/lib/timerUtils.ts` — NEW
- `src/lib/phaseConstants.ts` — NEW
- `src/components/sprint/SprintTimer.tsx` — remove local formatTime, add import
- `src/pages/AdminDisplay.tsx` — remove local formatTime + phases array, add imports
- `src/components/admin/TeamProgressCard.tsx` — remove local phaseLabels, add import
- `src/components/sprint/PhaseChecklist.tsx` — remove local phaseOrder, add import
- `src/hooks/useAIGeneration.ts` — add cooldown logic
- `src/data/sprintTasks.ts` — enrich task objects with guidance fields
- `supabase/functions/generate-startup-content/index.ts` — add rate limiter

## NOT in scope
- Gemini API integration (future session)
- Batch AI reviews for admin (future session)
- Auth migration to Supabase Auth (future, before residency)
- Automation track task enrichment (separate track)

## Verification
- Run `npm run dev`, navigate sprint phases — timer displays correctly
- Open `/admin/display` — timer + phase dots render
- Open admin dashboard — team cards show correct phase labels/colors
- Click AI generate, then immediately again — see cooldown toast
- Check enriched tasks show helper text, warnings, checklists in sprint view

1→export type TaskInputType = "text" | "textarea" | "url" | "confirmation" | "ai-generated";
     2→
     3→export type GeneratorType =
     4→  | "mvp-scope"
     5→  | "lovable-prompt"
     6→  | "target-customer"
     7→  | "outreach-messages"
     8→  | "customer-segments"
     9→  | "single-segment"
    10→  | "linkedin-search"
    11→  | "segment-outreach"
    12→  | "automation-analysis";
    13→
    14→export interface AIConfig {
    15→  generatorType: GeneratorType;
    16→  requiredPhase1Tasks: string[];
    17→  showCopyButton?: boolean;
    18→}
    19→
    20→export interface SprintTask {
    21→  key: string;
    22→  title: string;
    23→  description?: string;
    24→  exampleText?: string;
    25→  warningText?: string;
    26→  helperText?: string;
    27→  toolLink?: { label: string; url: string };
    28→  inputType: TaskInputType;
    29→  placeholder?: string;
    30→  required?: boolean;
    31→  optional?: boolean;
    32→  aiConfig?: AIConfig;
    33→  recommended?: boolean;
    34→  subNote?: string;
    35→  checklist?: string[];
    36→}
    37→
    38→export interface PhaseData {
    39→  phase: string;
    40→  label: string;
    41→  description: string;
    42→  phaseNote?: string;
    43→  tasks: SprintTask[];
    44→}
    45→
    46→const requiredPhase1Tasks = ["p1_problem", "p1_solution", "p1_customer"];
    47→
    48→export const sprintPhases: PhaseData[] = [
    49→  {
    50→    phase: "phase_1",
    51→    label: "PHASE 1: Idea & Business Model",
    52→    description: "60 minutes - Define your problem and solution",
    53→    tasks: [
    54→      {
    55→        key: "p1_problem",
    56→        title: "Define the problem you're solving",
    57→        description: "Write a clear problem statement in 1-2 sentences",
    58→        exampleText:
    59→          'Example: "People struggle to find healthy meal options that fit their dietary restrictions when ordering takeout"',
    60→        inputType: "textarea",
    61→        placeholder: "People struggle with...",
    62→        required: true,
    63→      },
    64→      {
    65→        key: "p1_solution",
    66→        title: "Describe your solution",

    68→        exampleText:

    70→        inputType: "textarea",

    72→        required: true,
    73→      },
    74→      {

    77→        description: "Who has this problem most acutely?",
    78→        exampleText:

    80→        inputType: "textarea",
    81→        placeholder: "Our target customer is...",

    83→      },
    84→      {
    85→        key: "p1_advantage",

    89→        inputType: "textarea",
    90→        placeholder: "Our unfair advantage is...",
    91→        required: true,
    92→      },

    97→        exampleText:

    99→        inputType: "textarea",
   100→        placeholder: "1. Evidence: ...\n2. Evidence: ...\n3. Evidence: ...",

content.text:
Perfect! I've successfully read all 7 files. Here's a summary of what I found:

## File Contents Summary

### 1. **SprintTimer.tsx**
A React component displaying a countdown timer with status badges and color-coded urgency indicators. Features:
- Formats seconds into HH:MM:SS format
- Shows status (LIVE, PAUSED, READY) with color-coded badges
- Color changes based on remaining time: green (>30min), yellow (10-30min), red (<10min, pulsing <1min)
- Displays urgent warnings for final minutes

### 2. **AdminDisplay.tsx**
A fullscreen display page for showing sprint progress to an audience. Features:
- Large timer display with status
- Phase progress indicators with visual dots showing completion
- Auto-hiding cursor after 3 seconds of inactivity
- Fullscreen toggle with 'F' key
- Shows app link, promo code, and WhatsApp QR code
- Phase progress with checkmarks for completed phases

### 3. **TeamProgressCard.tsx**
Expandable card component showing team progress tracking. Features:
- Displays team name, track (Startup/Automation), members, and last activity
- Collapsible phase breakdown showing task completion
- Progress bar and task counter
- Phase-based badges and status indicators
- Integration with TeamManagementDialog for team modifications
- Markdown rendering of task content

### 4. **PhaseChecklist.tsx**
Main checklist component for sprint phases with task management. Features:
- Expandable phase sections with colored headers
- Mix of manual input tasks and AI-generated tasks
- Task completion tracking with visual indicators
- Confetti celebration when phases complete
- Disabled state for future phases
- Supports different task input types (text, textarea, URL, confirmation, AI-generated)

### 5. **useAIGeneration.ts**
Custom React hook for AI content generation. Features:
- Calls Supabase edge function `generate-startup-content`
- Supports multiple generator types (MVP scope, prompts, customer segments, LinkedIn search, automation analysis)
- Handles error states and user notifications
- Manages loading state during generation
- Passes phase1Data, segmentData, and searchFilters to the backend

### 6. **sprintTasks.ts**
Data structure defining all sprint phases and tasks. Features:
- 4 main phases: Phase 1 (Idea, 60min), Phase 2 (Build & Discovery, 90min), Phase 3 (Demo & Pitch, 20min), Phase 4 (Extra Mile, optional)
- Each phase contains multiple tasks with different input types
- AI-generated tasks with configurations specifying generator types and required phase 1 tasks
- Optional and recommended task flags
- Tool links and example text for guidance

### 7. **generate-startup-content/index.ts** (Supabase Edge Function)
Backend AI content generation function. Features:
- Deno-based serverless function
- Routes different generator types to appropriate system prompts
- Calls Google Gemini 3 Flash Preview via Lovable's AI gateway
- Generates content for: MVP scope, Lovable prompts, target customers, outreach messages, customer segments, LinkedIn searches, automation analysis
- Supports both startup and automation tracks
- Error handling for rate limits and quota exhaustion

All files are well-structured with clear separation of concerns between UI components, state management, data definitions, and backend services.

content.text:
All 5 changes are implemented and the project builds cleanly. Here's a summary:

### Changes completed

1. **`src/lib/timerUtils.ts`** (new) — Shared `formatTime(seconds)` extracted from SprintTimer and AdminDisplay
2. **`src/lib/phaseConstants.ts`** (new) — Shared `phaseLabels`, `phaseNames`, `phaseOrder` extracted from TeamProgressCard, AdminDisplay, and PhaseChecklist
3. **`src/hooks/useAIGeneration.ts`** — Added 10-second client-side cooldown with `useRef` + toast warning
4. **`src/data/sprintTasks.ts`** — Enriched 10 tasks across all 4 phases with `helperText`, `warningText`, `checklist`, `subNote` fields (components already render these)
5. **`supabase/functions/generate-startup-content/index.ts`** — Added in-memory rate limiter (20 req/hour per auth token) with 429 response

**Files modified:** SprintTimer.tsx, AdminDisplay.tsx, TeamProgressCard.tsx, PhaseChecklist.tsx, useAIGeneration.ts, sprintTasks.ts, generate-startup-content/index.ts

**Type check:** Clean (0 errors) | **Build:** Successful

message.content: so how can i see & test it?

content.thinking: The user wants to test the changes locally.

content.text:
Run the dev server:

```bash
cd ~/habitatv5 && npm run dev
```

Then open these URLs to test each change:

1. **Timer + phases** — `http://localhost:5173` (or whatever port Vite shows) — navigate through sprint phases, check the timer displays correctly
2. **Admin display** — `http://localhost:5173/admin/display` — timer + phase dots should render
3. **Admin dashboard** — `http://localhost:5173/admin` — team cards show phase labels/colors
4. **AI cooldown** — Click any "Generate" button on a sprint task, then immediately click it again — you should see a toast like "Please wait 8s before generating again"
5. **Enriched tasks** — Go through Phase 1-4 sprint tasks — you'll see new helper text, warnings, and checklists on problem, solution, customer, advantage, validation, prototype, message, contacts, pitch, and social tasks

The server-side rate limit (change 5) only kicks in on the deployed Supabase edge function — you'd need to deploy it with `supabase functions deploy generate-startup-content` to test the 20 req/hour limit. Want me to start the dev server for you?

1→# Welcome to your Lovable project
     2→
     3→## Project info
     4→
     5→**URL**: https://lovable.dev/projects/REPLACE_WITH_PROJECT_ID
     6→
     7→## How can I edit this code?
     8→
     9→There are several ways of editing your application.
    10→
    11→**Use Lovable**
    12→
    13→Simply visit the [Lovable Project](https://lovable.dev/projects/REPLACE_WITH_PROJECT_ID) and start prompting.
    14→
    15→Changes made via Lovable will be committed automatically to this repo.
    16→
    17→**Use your preferred IDE**
    18→
    19→If you want to work locally using your own IDE, you can clone this repo and push changes. Pushed changes will also be reflected in Lovable.
    20→
    21→The only requirement is having Node.js & npm installed - [install with nvm](https://github.com/nvm-sh/nvm#installing-and-updating)
    22→
    23→Follow these steps:
    24→
    25→```sh
    26→# Step 1: Clone the repository using the project's Git URL.
    27→git clone <YOUR_GIT_URL>
    28→
    29→# Step 2: Navigate to the project directory.
    30→cd <YOUR_PROJECT_NAME>
    31→
    32→# Step 3: Install the necessary dependencies.
    33→npm i
    34→
    35→# Step 4: Start the development server with auto-reloading and an instant preview.
    36→npm run dev
    37→```
    38→
    39→**Edit a file directly in GitHub**
    40→
    41→- Navigate to the desired file(s).
    42→- Click the "Edit" button (pencil icon) at the top right of the file view.
    43→- Make your changes and commit the changes.
    44→
    45→**Use GitHub Codespaces**
    46→
    47→- Navigate to the main page of your repository.
    48→- Click on the "Code" button (green button) near the top right.
    49→- Select the "Codespaces" tab.
    50→- Click on "New codespace" to launch a new Codespace environment.
    51→- Edit files directly within the Codespace and commit and push your changes once you're done.
    52→
    53→## What technologies are used for this project?
    54→
    55→This project is built with:
    56→
    57→- Vite
    58→- TypeScript
    59→- React
    60→- shadcn-ui
    61→- Tailwind CSS
    62→
    63→## How can I deploy this project?
    64→
    65→Simply open [Lovable](https://lovable.dev/projects/REPLACE_WITH_PROJECT_ID) and click on Share -> Publish.
    66→
    67→## Can I connect a custom domain to my Lovable project?
    68→
    69→Yes, you can!
    70→
    71→To connect a domain, navigate to Project > Settings > Domains and click Connect Domain.
    72→
    73→Read more here: [Setting up a custom domain](https://docs.lovable.dev/features/custom-domain#custom-domain)
    74→

<system-reminder>

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

     8→

    10→  // Allows to automatically instantiate createClient with right options
    11→  // instead of createClient<Database, { PostgrestVersion: 'XX' }>(URL, KEY)

    14→  }
    15→  public: {

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me create a detailed summary report.

## DETAILED FINDINGS: Habitatv5 Project Coupling to Lovable & Supabase

Based on my thorough investigation of the codebase, here's a comprehensive analysis:

---

### 1. LOVABLE-SPECIFIC DEPENDENCIES & INTEGRATION

**Package.json:**
- `lovable-tagger` (^1.1.13) - Development-only dependency for tagging components used in Lovable's IDE

**Vite Config (vite.config.ts):**
- Lovable-specific plugin: `componentTagger()` is loaded in development mode
- Used only when `mode === "development"` to tag React components for Lovable's visual builder
- Lines 4 & 12: Imports and applies the component tagger plugin

**Lovable AI Generation Integration:**
- **Location:** `supabase/functions/generate-startup-content/index.ts` (line 437)
- **API Endpoint:** `https://ai.gateway.lovable.dev/v1/chat/completions`
- **Authentication:** Uses `LOVABLE_API_KEY` (Deno environment variable)
- **Model Used:** `google/gemini-3-flash-preview`
- **Rate Limiting:** 20 requests per hour per auth token (implemented in the edge function)
- **Purpose:** Generates AI-powered content for startup ideas, MVP scopes, customer segments, outreach messages, and automation analysis
- **Only AI mechanism:** This is the SOLE way AI generation works in the application—there's no fallback or alternative AI provider

**Lovable URLs & References:**
- README indicates deployment happens through Lovable's platform: `https://lovable.dev/projects/REPLACE_WITH_PROJECT_ID`
- Hardcoded Lovable link in task data: `https://lovable.dev/?via=habitat`
- README mentions editing via Lovable IDE and publishing through Lovable's Share feature

---

### 2. SUPABASE-SPECIFIC CONFIGURATION & INTEGRATION

**Environment Variables (from .env):**
```
VITE_SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID="zdbyetatbrlykkvrobhk"
VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY="eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6InpkYnlldGF0YnJseWtrdnJvYmhrIiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJpYXQiOjE3NzE4NTgxNDgsImV4cCI6MjA4NzQzNDE0OH0.ERos7rtYIXikTYde7B8SRRlN2A1aMbSl4yBf2__cZ9U"
VITE_SUPABASE_URL="https://zdbyetatbrlykkvrobhk.supabase.co"
```
- All using `VITE_` prefix (frontend-accessible environment variables)
- These are used directly in the browser client
- Publishable key is a JWT token (anon role)

**Client Configuration (src/integrations/supabase/client.ts):**
- Uses `@supabase/supabase-js` v2.90.1
- Loads credentials from `import.meta.env` (Vite environment variables)
- Configures local storage-based session persistence
- Auth setup: `localStorage`, `persistSession: true`, `autoRefreshToken: true`
- Automatically generated file (auto-synced with Supabase backend)

**Supabase Config (supabase/config.toml):**
- `project_id = "zdbyetatbrlykkvrobhk"` - hardcoded project ID

**Database Schema (TypeScript types auto-generated):**
- Tables: `customer_segments`, `event_registrations`, `events`, `profiles`, `teams`, `users`, `user_roles`, etc.
- Enums: `sprint_phase`, `event_status`
- Full PostgreSQL database with relationships, RLS policies

**Supabase Edge Functions:**
- Location: `supabase/functions/generate-startup-content/`
- Deno-based TypeScript function
- Callable via `supabase.functions.invoke('generate-startup-content')` from the frontend
- Handles AI generation requests with type, phase1Data, segmentData, searchFilters

**Database Migrations:**
- 5+ migration files in `supabase/migrations/`
- Full schema dump from PostgreSQL v17.6
- Includes auth extensions, RLS policies, tables, triggers

**Authentication:**
- Dual auth system:
  1. **Admin Auth:** Supabase built-in authentication (email/password)
  2. **Team Auth:** Custom implementation using Supabase DB (password hash storage in `teams` table)
- Admin auth checks `user_roles` table for admin permissions
- Team auth stores sessions in browser sessionStorage

---

### 3. ALL ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES USED

| Variable | Used By | Type | Required |
|----------|---------|------|----------|
| `VITE_SUPABASE_URL` | Frontend client | String | Yes |
| `VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` | Frontend client | JWT (anon) | Yes |
| `VITE_SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID` | Frontend (optional) | String | Implicit (in URL) |
| `LOVABLE_API_KEY` | Supabase edge function | String | Yes (for AI features) |

**Where used:**
- Frontend: Vite loads `VITE_*` variables automatically, accessible via `import.meta.env`
- Edge Function: Deno accesses via `Deno.env.get()` after deployment to Supabase

---

### 4. DEPENDENCY ANALYSIS

**Lovable-specific (can be removed):**
- `lovable-tagger` npm package
- `componentTagger()` plugin in vite.config.ts
- README mentions of Lovable project URL

**Supabase-required (core functionality):**
- `@supabase/supabase-js` (data, auth, edge functions)
- Database schema & migrations
- RLS policies (PostgreSQL)
- Edge function runtime (Deno)

**AI Generation (Lovable-coupled):**
- The Lovable API gateway endpoint is hardcoded
- `LOVABLE_API_KEY` is the only way to enable AI features
- No alternative AI provider integration exists

---

### 5. WHAT WOULD NEED TO CHANGE FOR INDEPENDENT OPERATION

**To run WITHOUT Lovable:**
1. Remove `lovable-tagger` from package.json devDependencies
2. Remove componentTagger import and plugin from vite.config.ts
3. Remove Lovable URLs from README and task data
4. Update vite.config.ts line 12 to just: `plugins: [react()]`

**To run WITHOUT Supabase:**
1. Replace `@supabase/supabase-js` with a different backend (Firebase, custom API, etc.)
2. Rewrite database queries in:
   - `src/contexts/TeamAuthContext.tsx` (team creation/login)
   - `src/contexts/AdminAuthContext.tsx` (admin auth)
   - All hooks using `supabase.from()` and `supabase.functions.invoke()`
3. Implement replacement for edge functions (could be Vercel functions, AWS Lambda, etc.)
4. Migrate database schema to new backend
5. Rewrite all RLS policies

**To replace Lovable AI with alternative provider:**
1. In `supabase/functions/generate-startup-content/index.ts`:
   - Change endpoint from `https://ai.gateway.lovable.dev/v1/chat/completions` to your AI provider
   - Change API key from `LOVABLE_API_KEY` to your provider's key
   - Update request/response mapping if using different API format
   - Change model string if needed
2. Options: OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, local LLM, etc.

---

### 6. CRITICAL HARDCODING ISSUES

**SECURITY CONCERN - Exposed credentials in .env:**
```
VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY contains a valid JWT token
VITE_SUPABASE_URL is a public Supabase project URL
```
These are intentionally public (anon role), but the specific project is identifiable.

**Hardcoded Project ID:**
- Supabase project ID `zdbyetatbrlykkvrobhk` appears in:
  - `.env` file
  - `supabase/config.toml`
  - Supabase client initialization
  - README

**Hardcoded Lovable API Endpoint:**
- No environment variable for the gateway URL
- Only `LOVABLE_API_KEY` is configurable
- API endpoint is locked to `https://ai.gateway.lovable.dev/v1/chat/completions`

---

### 7. DEPLOYMENT ARCHITECTURE

1. **Frontend (React/Vite):**
   - Served from Lovable's platform OR custom deployment
   - Communicates with Supabase via REST API

2. **Backend/Database (Supabase):**
   - PostgreSQL database hosted on Supabase
   - Authentication via Supabase Auth service
   - Edge functions run on Deno runtime

3. **AI Layer (Lovable API):**
   - Called from Supabase edge function
   - Requires `LOVABLE_API_KEY` to be set as secret in Supabase

---

### SUMMARY TABLE: DECOUPLING DIFFICULTY

| Component | Difficulty | Effort | Why |
|-----------|-----------|--------|-----|
| Remove Lovable IDE tagging | Easy | 5 mins | Just remove plugin & npm package |
| Replace Supabase for auth | Medium | 2-4 hours | Need to rewrite auth contexts |
| Replace Supabase database | Hard | 1-2 days | Need schema migration + all queries |
| Replace Lovable AI | Easy-Medium | 1-2 hours | Just change endpoint & key in edge function |
| Run fully independent | Very Hard | 3-5 days | Requires new backend, new AI provider, schema redesign |

---

This project is **tightly coupled to both Lovable and Supabase** at multiple levels: the development experience (Lovable IDE), the deployment platform (Lovable), the entire backend infrastructure (Supabase), and the AI capabilities (Lovable API gateway). The cleanest path to independence would be a gradual migration: swap out AI provider first (easiest), then consider backend replacement, and finally remove IDE/development-time dependencies.

Here's the practical picture:

### Your code is workable — keep Supabase, ditch Lovable

**Supabase is your real backend** — database, auth, edge functions. You should keep it connected. The `.env` already has your Supabase project credentials, so the app works against your existing database as-is.

**Lovable is only involved in 2 places**, both easy to remove:

| What | Where | Fix |
|------|-------|-----|
| `lovable-tagger` dev plugin | `vite.config.ts` + `package.json` | Remove plugin + package |
| AI gateway URL | Edge function (`generate-startup-content`) | Swap to direct Gemini/OpenAI endpoint |

### What I'd recommend

**Step 1 — Run it now (5 min)**
```bash
cd ~/habitatv5 && npm run dev
```
It will connect to your existing Supabase project using the `.env` credentials. All data, teams, auth — already there.

**Step 2 — Remove Lovable dev tooling (5 min)**
Strip `lovable-tagger` from `package.json` and `componentTagger()` from `vite.config.ts`. Purely cosmetic change.

**Step 3 — Replace AI provider (1-2 hours)**
The edge function hits `https://ai.gateway.lovable.dev/v1/chat/completions` with a `LOVABLE_API_KEY`. You'd swap that to call Gemini or OpenAI directly — the request format is already OpenAI-compatible, so it's mostly just changing the URL and key.

**Step 4 — Deploy independently**
Host the frontend on Vercel/Netlify/Cloudflare (it's just a Vite build). Supabase stays as your backend — no migration needed.

### What NOT to change

1→-- Enable realtime for teams and task_progress tables
     2→ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE public.teams;
     3→ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE public.task_progress;
     4→
     5→-- Add RLS policy for admins to view all teams
     6→CREATE POLICY "Admins can view all teams"
     7→ON public.teams
     8→FOR SELECT
     9→TO authenticated
    10→USING (public.has_role(auth.uid(), 'admin'));
    11→
    12→-- Add RLS policy for admins to view all task progress
    13→CREATE POLICY "Admins can view all task progress"
    14→ON public.task_progress
    15→FOR SELECT
    16→TO authenticated
    17→USING (public.has_role(auth.uid(), 'admin'));

content.content:
1→-- Create a trigger function to auto-assign admin role to first user
     2→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.assign_first_admin()
     3→RETURNS TRIGGER
     4→LANGUAGE plpgsql
     5→SECURITY DEFINER
     6→SET search_path = public
     7→AS $$
     8→BEGIN
     9→    -- Check if there are no existing admins
    10→    IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.user_roles WHERE role = 'admin') THEN
    11→        -- Assign admin role to this user
    12→        INSERT INTO public.user_roles (user_id, role)
    13→        VALUES (NEW.id, 'admin');
    14→    END IF;
    15→    RETURN NEW;
    16→END;
    17→$$;
    18→
    19→-- Create trigger on auth.users to fire after insert
    20→CREATE TRIGGER on_auth_user_created_assign_admin
    21→AFTER INSERT ON auth.users
    22→FOR EACH ROW
    23→EXECUTE FUNCTION public.assign_first_admin();

content.content:
1→-- Create sprint_timer table for global timer state
     2→CREATE TABLE public.sprint_timer (
     3→  id UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
     4→  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'stopped' CHECK (status IN ('stopped', 'running', 'paused')),
     5→  duration_seconds INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 14400, -- 4 hours
     6→  started_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
     7→  paused_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
     8→  elapsed_before_pause INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
     9→  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
    10→);
    11→
    12→-- Enable RLS
    13→ALTER TABLE public.sprint_timer ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    14→
    15→-- Everyone can view the timer
    16→CREATE POLICY "Everyone can view timer"
    17→ON public.sprint_timer
    18→FOR SELECT
    19→USING (true);
    20→
    21→-- Only admins can update timer
    22→CREATE POLICY "Admins can update timer"
    23→ON public.sprint_timer
    24→FOR UPDATE
    25→USING (
    26→  EXISTS (
    27→    SELECT 1 FROM public.user_roles
    28→    WHERE user_roles.user_id = auth.uid()
    29→    AND user_roles.role = 'admin'
    30→  )
    31→);
    32→
    33→-- Insert the singleton timer row
    34→INSERT INTO public.sprint_timer (id) VALUES ('00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001');
    35→
    36→-- Enable realtime for the timer table
    37→ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE public.sprint_timer;
    38→
    39→-- Create function to update timestamps
    40→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.update_sprint_timer_updated_at()
    41→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
    42→BEGIN
    43→  NEW.updated_at = now();
    44→  RETURN NEW;
    45→END;
    46→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SET search_path = public;
    47→
    48→-- Create trigger for automatic timestamp updates
    49→CREATE TRIGGER update_sprint_timer_updated_at
    50→BEFORE UPDATE ON public.sprint_timer
    51→FOR EACH ROW
    52→EXECUTE FUNCTION public.update_sprint_timer_updated_at();

1→-- Create table for storing team member names
     2→CREATE TABLE public.team_member_names (
     3→  id UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
     4→  team_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.teams(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
     5→  name TEXT NOT NULL,
     6→  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
     7→);
     8→
     9→-- Enable RLS
    10→ALTER TABLE public.team_member_names ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    11→
    12→-- Anyone can view team member names (for admin display purposes)
    13→CREATE POLICY "Anyone can view team member names"
    14→ON public.team_member_names
    15→FOR SELECT
    16→USING (true);
    17→
    18→-- Anyone can insert team member names (during team creation)
    19→CREATE POLICY "Anyone can insert team member names"
    20→ON public.team_member_names
    21→FOR INSERT
    22→WITH CHECK (true);
    23→
    24→-- Create index for faster lookups by team
    25→CREATE INDEX idx_team_member_names_team_id ON public.team_member_names(team_id);

content.content:
1→-- Create storage bucket for display images
     2→INSERT INTO storage.buckets (id, name, public)
     3→VALUES ('display-images', 'display-images', true)
     4→ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
     5→
     6→-- Allow anyone to view display images (public bucket)
     7→CREATE POLICY "Public can view display images"
     8→ON storage.objects FOR SELECT
     9→USING (bucket_id = 'display-images');
    10→
    11→-- Allow admins to upload display images
    12→CREATE POLICY "Admins can upload display images"
    13→ON storage.objects FOR INSERT
    14→WITH CHECK (
    15→  bucket_id = 'display-images' 
    16→  AND public.has_role(auth.uid(), 'admin')
    17→);
    18→
    19→-- Allow admins to update display images
    20→CREATE POLICY "Admins can update display images"

content.text:
Perfect! I now have all the information. Let me compile a comprehensive report of the complete Supabase setup.

## COMPLETE HABITATV5 SUPABASE SETUP DOCUMENTATION

### **PROJECT OVERVIEW**
- **Supabase Project ID:** `zdbyetatbrlykkvrobhk`
- **Database Version:** PostgreSQL 17.6
- **Postgrest Version:** 14.1

---

## **1. MIGRATION FILES (13 MIGRATIONS IN ORDER)**

### Migration 1: `20260111161120_remix_migration_from_pg_dump.sql`
**Full Contents:**
This is the primary database dump containing all core schema, types, functions, tables, constraints, indexes, triggers, and RLS policies. Key elements:

**Extensions:**
- pg_graphql
- pg_stat_statements
- pgcrypto
- plpgsql
- supabase_vault
- uuid-ossp

**Custom Types (ENUMs):**
```sql
CREATE TYPE public.app_role AS ENUM ('admin', 'participant');
CREATE TYPE public.event_status AS ENUM ('draft', 'scheduled', 'active', 'paused', 'completed');
CREATE TYPE public.sprint_phase AS ENUM ('waiting', 'phase_1', 'phase_2', 'phase_3', 'phase_4', 'completed');
```

**Functions:**
1. `handle_new_user()` - TRIGGER FUNCTION - Automatically creates a profile when a new user signs up
2. `has_role(_user_id uuid, _role app_role)` - Checks if user has a specific role
3. `is_team_member(_user_id uuid, _team_id uuid)` - Checks if user is a team member
4. `update_updated_at_column()` - Trigger function to auto-update `updated_at` timestamps

**Core Tables:**
1. **customer_segments** (id, team_id, name, company_type, job_titles[], why_they_care, is_custom, created_at, updated_at)
2. **event_registrations** (id, event_id, user_id, registered_at)
3. **events** (id, title, description, scheduled_date, status, current_phase, phase_started_at, created_by, created_at, updated_at)
4. **profiles** (id, user_id, full_name, avatar_url, created_at, updated_at)
5. **task_progress** (id, team_id, phase, task_key, completed, completed_by, completed_at, content, content_type, created_at, updated_at)
6. **team_deliverables** (id, team_id, type, title, url, notes, created_by, created_at, updated_at)
7. **team_members** (id, team_id, user_id, joined_at)
8. **teams** (id, event_id, name, invite_code, created_by, created_at, updated_at, password_hash)
9. **user_roles** (id, user_id, role, created_at)

**Constraints:**
- Unique indexes on: event_registrations(event_id, user_id), profiles(user_id), task_progress(team_id, phase, task_key), team_members(team_id, user_id), teams(invite_code), user_roles(user_id, role)
- Unique index on teams(name, event_id)

**Foreign Keys:**
- All major table relationships defined with ON DELETE CASCADE/SET NULL as appropriate

**RLS Policies (25 total):**
- Admins: Can manage events, roles, view all registrations, update/delete teams, delete task progress
- Creators: Can manage own events, update own teams
- Team Members: Can add deliverables, modify/view task progress, update/view deliverables, view members, view team segments
- Users: Can insert own profile, join/leave teams, register for events, update own profile, view all profiles, view own registrations/roles
- Public: Can view scheduled events, view teams, view task progress, insert/update/delete task progress

**RLS Enabled on:** customer_segments, event_registrations, events, profiles, task_progress, team_deliverables, team_members, teams, user_roles

**Triggers:**
- update_customer_segments_updated_at
- update_events_updated_at
- update_profiles_updated_at
- update_task_progress_updated_at
- update_team_deliverables_updated_at
- update_teams_updated_at

---

### Migration 2: `20260111185414_76e1ed32-b69f-417b-91dc-3be66aad03d8.sql`

**Contents:**
```sql
ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE public.teams;
ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE public.task_progress;

CREATE POLICY "Admins can view all teams"
ON public.teams FOR SELECT TO authenticated
USING (public.has_role(auth.uid(), 'admin'));

CREATE POLICY "Admins can view all task progress"
ON public.task_progress FOR SELECT TO authenticated
USING (public.has_role(auth.uid(), 'admin'));
```
**Purpose:** Enable realtime subscriptions for teams and task_progress tables; add admin viewing policies.

---

### Migration 3: `20260111211211_cb491fb8-c7bb-42ee-b0af-596d81628e44.sql`

**Contents:**
```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.assign_first_admin()
RETURNS TRIGGER
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
BEGIN
    IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.user_roles WHERE role = 'admin') THEN
        INSERT INTO public.user_roles (user_id, role)
        VALUES (NEW.id, 'admin');
    END IF;
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;

CREATE TRIGGER on_auth_user_created_assign_admin
AFTER INSERT ON auth.users
FOR EACH ROW
EXECUTE FUNCTION public.assign_first_admin();
```
**Purpose:** Auto-assign admin role to the first user to sign up.

---

### Migration 4: `20260112202049_1e135871-04ec-496a-848d-58979a3ce16b.sql`

**Contents:**
```sql
CREATE POLICY "Admins can update all teams"
ON public.teams FOR UPDATE
USING (has_role(auth.uid(), 'admin'::app_role));

CREATE POLICY "Admins can delete all teams"
ON public.teams FOR DELETE
USING (has_role(auth.uid(), 'admin'::app_role));

CREATE POLICY "Admins can delete all task progress"
ON public.task_progress FOR DELETE
USING (has_role(auth.uid(), 'admin'::app_role));
```
**Purpose:** Add admin management policies for teams and task_progress.

---

### Migration 5: `20260113101305_1e65c74c-d03d-4313-8c7b-15a27f76d12f.sql`

**Contents:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE public.sprint_timer (
  id UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'stopped' CHECK (status IN ('stopped', 'running', 'paused')),
  duration_seconds INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 14400,
  started_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  paused_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  elapsed_before_pause INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

ALTER TABLE public.sprint_timer ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "Everyone can view timer"
ON public.sprint_timer FOR SELECT USING (true);

CREATE POLICY "Admins can update timer"
ON public.sprint_timer FOR UPDATE
USING (EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 FROM public.user_roles
  WHERE user_roles.user_id = auth.uid()
  AND user_roles.role = 'admin'
));

INSERT INTO public.sprint_timer (id) VALUES ('00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001');

ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE public.sprint_timer;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.update_sprint_timer_updated_at()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  NEW.updated_at = now();
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SET search_path = public;

CREATE TRIGGER update_sprint_timer_updated_at
BEFORE UPDATE ON public.sprint_timer
FOR EACH ROW
EXECUTE FUNCTION public.update_sprint_timer_updated_at();
```
**Purpose:** Create sprint timer table for 4-hour countdown timer (14400 seconds). Singleton pattern with one row. Realtime enabled.

---

### Migration 6: `20260126150653_ac98e226-990f-4563-a5d2-e64130147704.sql`

**Contents:**
```sql
ALTER TABLE public.sprint_timer 
ADD COLUMN display_phase public.sprint_phase NOT NULL DEFAULT 'waiting',
ADD COLUMN promo_code text;
```
**Purpose:** Add display_phase and promo_code columns to sprint_timer for UI state and promotions.

---

### Migration 7: `20260126151937_a079573d-5f15-476b-b187-83a5798c8727.sql`

**Contents:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE public.team_member_names (
  id UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
  team_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.teams(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,

## **1. MIGRATION FILES (13 MIGRATIONS IN ORDER)**

**Full Contents:**
This is the primary database dump containing all core schema, types, functions, tables, constraints, indexes, triggers, and RLS policies. Key elements:

**Custom Types (ENUMs):**
```sql
CREATE TYPE public.app_role AS ENUM ('admin', 'participant');

CREATE TYPE public.sprint_phase AS ENUM ('waiting', 'phase_1', 'phase_2', 'phase_3', 'phase_4', 'completed');
```

**Functions:**
1. `handle_new_user()` - TRIGGER FUNCTION - Automatically creates a profile when a new user signs up
2. `has_role(_user_id uuid, _role app_role)` - Checks if user has a specific role
3. `is_team_member(_user_id uuid, _team_id uuid)` - Checks if user is a team member
4. `update_updated_at_column()` - Trigger function to auto-update `updated_at` timestamps

**Core Tables:**
1. **customer_segments** (id, team_id, name, company_type, job_titles[], why_they_care, is_custom, created_at, updated_at)
2. **event_registrations** (id, event_id, user_id, registered_at)
3. **events** (id, title, description, scheduled_date, status, current_phase, phase_started_at, created_by, created_at, updated_at)
4. **profiles** (id, user_id, full_name, avatar_url, created_at, updated_at)
5. **task_progress** (id, team_id, phase, task_key, completed, completed_by, completed_at, content, content_type, created_at, updated_at)
6. **team_deliverables** (id, team_id, type, title, url, notes, created_by, created_at, updated_at)
7. **team_members** (id, team_id, user_id, joined_at)
8. **teams** (id, event_id, name, invite_code, created_by, created_at, updated_at, password_hash)
9. **user_roles** (id, user_id, role, created_at)

**Constraints:**
- Unique indexes on: event_registrations(event_id, user_id), profiles(user_id), task_progress(team_id, phase, task_key), team_members(team_id, user_id), teams(invite_code), user_roles(user_id, role)
- Unique index on teams(name, event_id)

**Foreign Keys:**
- All major table relationships defined with ON DELETE CASCADE/SET NULL as appropriate

**RLS Policies (25 total):**
- Admins: Can manage events, roles, view all registrations, update/delete teams, delete task progress
- Creators: Can manage own events, update own teams
- Team Members: Can add deliverables, modify/view task progress, update/view deliverables, view members, view team segments
- Users: Can insert own profile, join/leave teams, register for events, update own profile, view all profiles, view own registrations/roles
- Public: Can view scheduled events, view teams, view task progress, insert/update/delete task progress

**RLS Enabled on:** customer_segments, event_registrations, events, profiles, task_progress, team_deliverables, team_members, teams, user_roles

- update_customer_segments_updated_at

- update_task_progress_updated_at
- update_team_deliverables_updated_at
- update_teams_updated_at

### Migration 3: `20260111211211_cb491fb8-c7bb-42ee-b0af-596d81628e44.sql`

**Contents:**
```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.assign_first_admin()
RETURNS TRIGGER
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
BEGIN
    IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.user_roles WHERE role = 'admin') THEN
        INSERT INTO public.user_roles (user_id, role)
        VALUES (NEW.id, 'admin');
    END IF;
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;

CREATE TRIGGER on_auth_user_created_assign_admin
AFTER INSERT ON auth.users
FOR EACH ROW
EXECUTE FUNCTION public.assign_first_admin();
```
**Purpose:** Auto-assign admin role to the first user to sign up.

**Function Purpose:** AI-powered content generation for startups using Lovable AI API gateway. Serves multiple generator types:
- MVP scope definition
- Lovable prompts
- Target customer profiles
- Outreach message generation
- Customer segmentation
- LinkedIn search queries
- Segment-specific outreach

**Key Features:**
- CORS-enabled HTTP endpoint
- Rate limiting: 20 requests per hour per auth token
- Integrates with Lovable AI gateway (`https://ai.gateway.lovable.dev/v1/chat/completions`)
- Uses Google Gemini 3 Flash model for content generation
- Requires `LOVABLE_API_KEY` environment variable

**Environment Variable:**

## **SUMMARY FOR FRESH SUPABASE SETUP**

To recreate this Supabase project from scratch:

1. **Run migrations in order** (13 migration files total)
2. **Create 3 custom ENUM types**: app_role, event_status, sprint_phase
3. **Create 10 tables**: customer_segments, event_registrations, events, profiles, sprint_timer, task_progress, team_deliverables, team_member_names, team_members, teams, user_roles
4. **Enable RLS** on all 11 tables with 25+ policies
5. **Create 4 trigger functions**: handle_new_user, assign_first_admin, update_updated_at_column, update_sprint_timer_updated_at
6. **Create storage bucket**: 'display-images' (public) with admin-only write policies
7. **Enable realtime** on: teams, task_progress, sprint_timer
8. **Initialize sprint_timer** with singleton row (id: '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001')
9. **Set environment variables**: VITE_SUPABASE_URL, VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, VITE_SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID, LOVABLE_API_KEY
10. **Deploy edge function**: generate-startup-content (Deno-based, uses Lovable AI API)

CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "pg_graphql";
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "pg_stat_statements" WITH SCHEMA "extensions";
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "pgcrypto" WITH SCHEMA "extensions";
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "plpgsql";
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "supabase_vault";
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "uuid-ossp" WITH SCHEMA "extensions";
BEGIN;

--
-- PostgreSQL database dump
--


-- Dumped from database version 17.6
-- Dumped by pg_dump version 18.1

SET statement_timeout = 0;
SET lock_timeout = 0;
SET idle_in_transaction_session_timeout = 0;
SET transaction_timeout = 0;
SET client_encoding = 'UTF8';
SET standard_conforming_strings = on;
SELECT pg_catalog.set_config('search_path', '', false);
SET check_function_bodies = false;
SET xmloption = content;
SET client_min_messages = warning;
SET row_security = off;

--
-- Name: public; Type: SCHEMA; Schema: -; Owner: -
--



--
-- Name: app_role; Type: TYPE; Schema: public; Owner: -
--

CREATE TYPE public.app_role AS ENUM (
    'admin',
    'participant'
);


--
-- Name: event_status; Type: TYPE; Schema: public; Owner: -
--

CREATE TYPE public.event_status AS ENUM (
    'draft',
    'scheduled',
    'active',
    'paused',
    'completed'
);


--
-- Name: sprint_phase; Type: TYPE; Schema: public; Owner: -
--

CREATE TYPE public.sprint_phase AS ENUM (
    'waiting',
    'phase_1',
    'phase_2',
    'phase_3',
    'phase_4',
    'completed'
);


--
-- Name: handle_new_user(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: public; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION public.handle_new_user() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    SET search_path TO 'public'
    AS $$
BEGIN
    INSERT INTO public.profiles (user_id, full_name)
    VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'full_name');
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: has_role(uuid, public.app_role); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: public; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION public.has_role(_user_id uuid, _role public.app_role) RETURNS boolean
    LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
    SET search_path TO 'public'
    AS $$
    SELECT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM public.user_roles
        WHERE user_id = _user_id AND role = _role
    )
$$;


--
-- Name: is_team_member(uuid, uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: public; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION public.is_team_member(_user_id uuid, _team_id uuid) RETURNS boolean
    LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
    SET search_path TO 'public'
    AS $$
    SELECT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM public.team_members
        WHERE user_id = _user_id AND team_id = _team_id
    )
$$;


--
-- Name: update_updated_at_column(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: public; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION public.update_updated_at_column() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    SET search_path TO 'public'
    AS $$
BEGIN
    NEW.updated_at = now();
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;


SET default_table_access_method = heap;

--
-- Name: customer_segments; Type: TABLE; Schema: public; Owner: -
--

CREATE TABLE public.customer_segments (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    team_id uuid NOT NULL,
    name text NOT NULL,
    company_type text NOT NULL,
    job_titles text[] DEFAULT '{}'::text[] NOT NULL,
    why_they_care text NOT NULL,
    is_custom boolean DEFAULT false NOT NULL,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL
);


--
-- Name: event_registrations; Type: TABLE; Schema: public; Owner: -
--

CREATE TABLE public.event_registrations (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    event_id uuid NOT NULL,
    user_id uuid NOT NULL,
    registered_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL
);


--
-- Name: events; Type: TABLE; Schema: public; Owner: -
--

CREATE TABLE public.events (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    title text NOT NULL,
    description text,
    scheduled_date timestamp with time zone NOT NULL,
    status public.event_status DEFAULT 'draft'::public.event_status NOT NULL,
    current_phase public.sprint_phase DEFAULT 'waiting'::public.sprint_phase NOT NULL,
    phase_started_at timestamp with time zone,
    created_by uuid,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL
);


--
-- Name: profiles; Type: TABLE; Schema: public; Owner: -
--

CREATE TABLE public.profiles (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    user_id uuid NOT NULL,
    full_name text,
    avatar_url text,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL
);


--
-- Name: task_progress; Type: TABLE; Schema: public; Owner: -
--

CREATE TABLE public.task_progress (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    team_id uuid NOT NULL,
    phase public.sprint_phase NOT NULL,
    task_key text NOT NULL,
    completed boolean DEFAULT false NOT NULL,
    completed_by uuid,
    completed_at timestamp with time zone,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL,
    content text,
    content_type text DEFAULT 'text'::text
);


--
-- Name: team_deliverables; Type: TABLE; Schema: public; Owner: -
--

CREATE TABLE public.team_deliverables (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    team_id uuid NOT NULL,
    type text NOT NULL,
    title text NOT NULL,
    url text,
    notes text,
    created_by uuid,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL
);


--
-- Name: team_members; Type: TABLE; Schema: public; Owner: -
--

CREATE TABLE public.team_members (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    team_id uuid NOT NULL,
    user_id uuid NOT NULL,
    joined_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL
);


--
-- Name: teams; Type: TABLE; Schema: public; Owner: -
--

CREATE TABLE public.teams (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    event_id uuid,
    name text NOT NULL,
    invite_code text DEFAULT "substring"(md5((random())::text), 1, 8),
    created_by uuid,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL,
    password_hash text
);


--
-- Name: user_roles; Type: TABLE; Schema: public; Owner: -
--

CREATE TABLE public.user_roles (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    user_id uuid NOT NULL,
    role public.app_role NOT NULL,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL
);


--
-- Name: customer_segments customer_segments_pkey; Type: CONSTRAINT; Schema: public; Owner: -
--

ALTER TABLE ONLY public.customer_segments

input.content:
# Migrate habitatv5 off Lovable → Fresh Supabase + Direct Gemini

## Context
Habitat's sprint framework currently depends on Lovable for two things: (1) a dev-only component tagger plugin, and (2) the AI gateway that proxies Gemini API calls. The goal is to remove all Lovable dependencies, set up a fresh Supabase project, swap the AI edge function to call Google Gemini directly, and update the `.env` to point at the new project.

## Part A: Remove Lovable dependencies (code changes)

### A1. Remove `lovable-tagger` from vite config
- **File:** `vite.config.ts`
- Remove `import { componentTagger } from "lovable-tagger"`
- Remove `mode === "development" && componentTagger()` from plugins array
- Remove the `.filter(Boolean)` (no longer needed)

### A2. Remove `lovable-tagger` from package.json
- **File:** `package.json`
- Remove `"lovable-tagger"` from `devDependencies`

### A3. Swap AI edge function to direct Google Gemini API
- **File:** `supabase/functions/generate-startup-content/index.ts`
- Change endpoint from `https://ai.gateway.lovable.dev/v1/chat/completions` → `https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai/chat/completions`
- Change env var from `LOVABLE_API_KEY` → `GEMINI_API_KEY`
- Change auth header from `Bearer ${LOVABLE_API_KEY}` → `Bearer ${GEMINI_API_KEY}`
- Change model from `google/gemini-3-flash-preview` → `gemini-2.0-flash`
- Error message update: "AI service not configured" stays, just references GEMINI_API_KEY

## Part B: Fresh Supabase project setup (step-by-step guide for user)

### B1. Create new Supabase project
- Go to https://supabase.com/dashboard → New Project
- Pick org, name, region, password → Create

### B2. Get credentials and update `.env`
- **File:** `.env`
- From Project Settings → API: copy URL, anon key, project ref
- Update `VITE_SUPABASE_URL`, `VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY`, `VITE_SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID`

### B3. Update `supabase/config.toml`
- Replace `project_id` with new project ref

### B4. Run consolidated migration SQL
- Generate a single clean SQL file combining all 13 migrations
- User runs it in Supabase SQL Editor (Dashboard → SQL Editor → New Query → paste → Run)

### B5. Enable Realtime
- The migration SQL includes `ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE` for teams, task_progress, sprint_timer

### B6. Create storage bucket
- The migration SQL includes the `display-images` bucket creation + RLS policies

### B7. Deploy edge function
- Link CLI: `supabase link --project-ref <new-ref>`
- Set secret: `supabase secrets set GEMINI_API_KEY=<your-key>`
- Deploy: `supabase functions deploy generate-startup-content`

### B8. Create admin user
- Sign up via the app (first user gets admin role automatically via trigger)
- Or: create user in Supabase Dashboard → Authentication → Add User

## Part C: Generate consolidated migration SQL
- Merge all 13 migration files into one clean SQL file at `supabase/migrations/00000000000000_full_schema.sql`
- Remove the 13 old migration files
- This gives a clean starting point for the new project

## Files Modified
- `vite.config.ts` — remove lovable-tagger
- `package.json` — remove lovable-tagger dep
- `supabase/functions/generate-startup-content/index.ts` — Gemini direct API
- `.env` — placeholder for new Supabase credentials
- `supabase/config.toml` — placeholder project_id
- `supabase/migrations/00000000000000_full_schema.sql` — NEW consolidated migration
- `supabase/migrations/*` — DELETE old 13 files

## Verification
1. `npm run dev` starts without errors (no lovable-tagger import failure)
2. `vite build` succeeds
3. After Supabase setup: app connects, team creation works, timer loads
4. After edge function deploy: AI generation calls Gemini directly
5. First signup gets admin role automatically

1→# habitatv5 Framework Quick Fixes + UX Improvements
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→Habitat's sprint framework (React + Supabase) runs during builder events. The eng review identified code duplication, missing AI rate limiting, and underutilized UX guidance fields. Admin display auth was confirmed already working — no change needed.
     5→
     6→## Changes (5 total, in order)
     7→
     8→### 1. Extract shared `formatTime` → `src/lib/timerUtils.ts` (new file)
     9→- Identical function duplicated in `SprintTimer.tsx:4-10` and `AdminDisplay.tsx:17-22`
    10→- Create `src/lib/timerUtils.ts` with exported `formatTime(seconds)`
    11→- Import in both files, delete local copies

    17→- Keep component-specific color maps in place (different formats: CSS classes vs Tailwind)

    24→### 4. Enrich sprint task guidance → `src/data/sprintTasks.ts` (data-only)

file.content:
# habitatv5 Framework Quick Fixes + UX Improvements

## Context
Habitat's sprint framework (React + Supabase) runs during builder events. The eng review identified code duplication, missing AI rate limiting, and underutilized UX guidance fields. Admin display auth was confirmed already working — no change needed.

## Changes (5 total, in order)

### 1. Extract shared `formatTime` → `src/lib/timerUtils.ts` (new file)
- Identical function duplicated in `SprintTimer.tsx:4-10` and `AdminDisplay.tsx:17-22`
- Create `src/lib/timerUtils.ts` with exported `formatTime(seconds)`
- Import in both files, delete local copies

### 2. Extract shared phase constants → `src/lib/phaseConstants.ts` (new file)
- `phaseLabels` duplicated in `TeamProgressCard.tsx:17-24` and implicitly in `AdminDisplay.tsx`
- Create `src/lib/phaseConstants.ts` with `phaseLabels`, `phaseNames`, `phaseOrder`
- Import in `TeamProgressCard.tsx`, `AdminDisplay.tsx`, `PhaseChecklist.tsx`
- Keep component-specific color maps in place (different formats: CSS classes vs Tailwind)

### 3. Client-side AI rate limiting → `src/hooks/useAIGeneration.ts`
- Add `useRef` for `lastGenerationTime`, enforce 10s cooldown
- Show toast "Please wait Xs before generating again" if too fast
- ~9 lines of new code at top of `generate()` function

### 4. Enrich sprint task guidance → `src/data/sprintTasks.ts` (data-only)
- Add `helperText`, `warningText`, `checklist`, `toolLink`, `subNote` to key tasks
- Components already render all these fields — zero component changes needed
- Phase 1: problem, solution, customer, advantage, validation enriched
- Phase 2: prototype, message, contacts enriched
- Phase 3: pitch enriched
- Phase 4: social enriched

### 5. Server-side AI rate limiting → `supabase/functions/generate-startup-content/index.ts`
- Add in-memory request counter (20 requests/hour per token)
- Insert rate check after body parsing, before API call (~25 lines)
- Returns 429 with friendly error message

## Files Modified
- `src/lib/timerUtils.ts` — NEW
- `src/lib/phaseConstants.ts` — NEW
- `src/components/sprint/SprintTimer.tsx` — remove local formatTime, add import
- `src/pages/AdminDisplay.tsx` — remove local formatTime + phases array, add imports
- `src/components/admin/TeamProgressCard.tsx` — remove local phaseLabels, add import
- `src/components/sprint/PhaseChecklist.tsx` — remove local phaseOrder, add import
- `src/hooks/useAIGeneration.ts` — add cooldown logic
- `src/data/sprintTasks.ts` — enrich task objects with guidance fields
- `supabase/functions/generate-startup-content/index.ts` — add rate limiter

## NOT in scope
- Gemini API integration (future session)
- Batch AI reviews for admin (future session)
- Auth migration to Supabase Auth (future, before residency)
- Automation track task enrichment (separate track)

## Verification
- Run `npm run dev`, navigate sprint phases — timer displays correctly
- Open `/admin/display` — timer + phase dots render
- Open admin dashboard — team cards show correct phase labels/colors
- Click AI generate, then immediately again — see cooldown toast
- Check enriched tasks show helper text, warnings, checklists in sprint view

input.content:
# Migrate habitatv5 off Lovable → Fresh Supabase + Direct Gemini

## Context
Habitat's sprint framework currently depends on Lovable for: (1) a dev-only component tagger plugin, and (2) the AI gateway that proxies Gemini API calls. The goal is to remove all Lovable dependencies, set up a fresh Supabase project, swap the AI edge function to call Google Gemini directly, and update the env to point at the new project.

---

## Part A: Remove Lovable from code (3 edits)

### A1. `vite.config.ts` — remove lovable-tagger
- Delete `import { componentTagger } from "lovable-tagger"`
- Change plugins to just `plugins: [react()]`

### A2. `package.json` — remove lovable-tagger dep
- Delete `"lovable-tagger"` from `devDependencies`

### A3. `supabase/functions/generate-startup-content/index.ts` — swap to direct Gemini
- Endpoint: `https://ai.gateway.lovable.dev/...` → `https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai/chat/completions`
- Env var: `LOVABLE_API_KEY` → `GEMINI_API_KEY`
- Model: `google/gemini-3-flash-preview` → `gemini-2.0-flash`
- (Google's OpenAI-compatible endpoint uses same request/response format — no other changes needed)

---

## Part B: Fresh Supabase setup (guided, step by step)

### B1. User creates new Supabase project
- https://supabase.com/dashboard → New Project
- Pick org, name, region, DB password → Create

### B2. Update `.env` with new credentials
- From Project Settings → API: copy Project URL, anon key, project ref
- Update `VITE_SUPABASE_URL`, `VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY`, `VITE_SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID`

### B3. Update `supabase/config.toml`
- Replace `project_id` with new project ref

### B4. Run consolidated migration SQL
- Merge all 13 existing migrations into one clean `supabase/migrations/00000000000000_full_schema.sql`
- Delete the 13 old migration files
- User pastes the SQL into Supabase Dashboard → SQL Editor → Run
- Includes: enums, functions, tables, constraints, indexes, triggers, RLS policies, realtime, storage bucket, seed data (singleton timer row)

### B5. Deploy edge function
```
supabase link --project-ref <new-ref>
supabase secrets set GEMINI_API_KEY=<your-google-ai-key>
supabase functions deploy generate-startup-content
```

### B6. Create admin user
- First user to sign up gets admin role automatically (via `assign_first_admin` trigger)

---

## Part C: Clean up old migration files
- Remove all 13 individual migration files from `supabase/migrations/`
- Single consolidated file replaces them

---

## Files Modified
- `vite.config.ts` — remove lovable-tagger plugin
- `package.json` — remove lovable-tagger from devDependencies
- `supabase/functions/generate-startup-content/index.ts` — Gemini direct API
- `.env` — placeholder values for new Supabase project
- `supabase/config.toml` — placeholder project_id
- `supabase/migrations/00000000000000_full_schema.sql` — NEW consolidated migration
- `supabase/migrations/2026*` — DELETE all 13 old files

## Verification
1. `npm run build` succeeds (no lovable-tagger errors)
2. After Supabase setup + migration: `npm run dev` → app loads, timer works
3. Sign up → automatically get admin role
4. Create team → shows in admin dashboard
5. AI generate → calls Gemini directly (after edge function deploy)
